01 — About Me

Building machines that
think & act


I'm Rohit, an international student from India pursuing my MSc in Autonomy Technologies at FAU Erlangen-Nurnberg — one of Germany's top technical universities. My focus is Robotics and AI, working at the intersection of control theory, machine learning, and real hardware.

My hands-on experience spans from torque-controlling a 7-DoF Franka Emika robot arm in the lab to building CNN-based disease detection systems. I love turning complex technical ideas into real, working solutions.

Actively looking for internship or working student positions in robotics, AI, or data science — ready to relocate anywhere in Germany.

🧞 Robot Programming 🧠 AI Research 📊 Data Storytelling 🏈 Motorcyclist ⛰ Trekker 🌍 Explorer 🍳 Home Cook 🎯 Detail-Oriented

03 — Experience

Where I've Worked



October 2025 — Present
Student Assistant — Robotics & Control Lab
FAU Erlangen · Erlangen, Germany
  • Implemented joint-space and Cartesian-space torque control on a 7-DoF Franka Emika robot using real-time interfaces.
  • Designed and validated PD, PID, impedance, and hybrid force/motion controllers, analyzing stability, damping, and tracking performance.
  • Applied model-based robot dynamics (inertia, Coriolis, gravity) to compare decentralized and centralized control strategies.
  • Designed MPC for constrained systems including modeling, discretization, and tuning.
  • Implemented digital state-space control including LQR design and experimental verification on real lab systems.
November 2024 — May 2025
Data Analytics Intern
Oorrmi Perfumes · Remote
  • Cleaned and structured large customer datasets to identify buying patterns and product preferences.
  • Built a Power BI dashboard covering 10,000+ entries with key trends, filters, and comparisons.
  • Identified high-value customer segments, improving targeting accuracy by approximately 20%.
  • Translated complex data into clear visuals enabling quick team decision-making.
January 2022 — February 2022
Machine Learning & OpenCV Intern
GB Softronics · Nashik, India
  • Engineered an AI-based anomaly detection system using Python and OpenCV, improving accuracy by 35%.
  • Designed image processing filters for Plant Leaf Disease Detection using ML and computer vision.
  • Translated complex ML concepts into real-time performance solutions using PyTorch and TensorFlow.
04 — Projects

Things I've Built



01
Plant Leaf Disease Detection — Thesis
CNNs with PyTorch/TensorFlow achieving 87% accuracy. Curated 5,000+ images with augmentation. Built a Python GUI for farmers. Published in an international journal.
CNNPyTorchTensorFlowOpenCVPublished
02
7-DoF Robot Arm Control — FAU Lab
Implemented PD, PID, impedance, and MPC controllers on a real Franka Emika robot arm. Analyzed stability using model-based robot dynamics.
RoboticsMPCROS2MATLABControl Theory

Customer Analytics Dashboard
Power BI dashboard from 10,000+ records. Identified high-value segments improving targeting accuracy by 20%. Clear visuals for non-technical stakeholders.

06 — Beyond the Lab

Life outside
the algorithm


The best engineers are also the most curious humans. When I'm not in the lab, you'll find me chasing horizons on a motorbike, summiting peaks, cooking from scratch, or exploring Germany and beyond.

⛰ Western Ghats, India
Chasing waterfalls — where engineers go to debug their minds.
🌍 Saxon Switzerland, Germany
Exploring my new home country. Germany never disappoints.
🏔 Alpine Adventures
Summit happiness — the Alps hit different when you've earned the view.
🏈 Moto Camping, Sahyadri
Rode 300km into the monsoon — tents, bikes, mountains. Perfect weekend.
🥾 Hilltop Trek
Summit reached. Breathing in the view. This is what recharges the battery.
🍳 Home Cooking, Erlangen
Butter chicken from scratch — precision in the lab, passion in the kitchen.
🏈
Motorcyclist
Long-distance rides across the Sahyadri mountains — built patience, resilience, and precision navigation.
⛰
Trekker
From Western Ghats to the Alps — I thrive when the path gets steep and the view demands effort.
🏕
Explorer
Living abroad, learning German, embracing new cultures — always stepping outside comfort zones.
🍳
Home Cook
Cooking from scratch is my way of mixing worlds — Indian spices, German kitchen, global palate.
I approach every mountain — whether it's a Ghats peak or a complex control algorithm — the same way: assess the terrain, plan the route, adapt on the climb, and never stop until there's a view worth sharing.
— ROHIT JADHAV
